justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timezone bug clearly identified — getHours() returns UTC hours on server, not customer-local hours. (2) Specific New York customer example with exact times (13:00 ET → 18:00 UTC → rejected despite being before 2 PM local cutoff). (3) Fix is specific: accept customer timezone identifier, use Intl.DateTimeFormat with timeZone option, includes working code example. (4) Severity marked Critical, appropriate for multi-timezone expansion. (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent specificity: reproduction steps include exact dates, times, and UTC conversions showing the math behind incorrect behavior
Identified the cross-midnight boundary bug (22:00 ET → 02:00 UTC next day) as a separate critical finding — goes beyond the minimum rubric requirements
DST analysis is thorough, noting that even London-only deployment is affected during BST (summer), not just non-UTC timezones
Fix includes concrete TypeScript code using Intl.DateTimeFormat and mentions date-fns-tz/luxon as alternatives
Notes the breaking change to function signature and that all callers need updating — practical deployment awareness
Correctly identifies that current London-only deployment is partially masked in winter but breaks during BST — nuanced severity assessment
